metadata.source: Literature review — systematic searches of PubMed, Google Scholar, medRxiv
metadata.notes: Effect sizes updated from CLAUDE.md defaults based on published evidence. Ranges reflect heterogeneity across study settings.

a0.label: No intervention
a0.effect_measure: absolute percentage point increase in completion
a0.notes: Baseline — no additional intervention beyond routine system

a1.label: SMS reminder
a1.effect_measure: absolute percentage point increase in completion
a1.evidence_source: Eze et al. 2021 meta-analysis (18 LMICs, 32712 infants); Mekonnen et al. 2021 Ethiopia RCT
a1.evidence_quality: High — systematic review + meta-analysis of 13 RCTs
a1.notes: CLAUDE.md default was +5-10%. Updated UPPER bound to 15% based on low-income subgroup RR=1.39. Effect stronger in low-income settings. More than 2 reminders significantly improves timeliness (p=0.040).

a2.label: CHW home visit
a2.effect_measure: absolute percentage point increase in completion
a2.evidence_source: Brugha et al. 2008 Ghana trial (+25pp); Meta-analysis of vaccination strategies 2025 (OR 3.31 for access enhancement); Multiple African RCTs
a2.evidence_quality: Moderate-High — multiple RCTs but heterogeneous designs
a2.notes: CLAUDE.md default was +15-25%. CONFIRMED by literature. Ghana trial showed 60%→85% coverage. Access enhancement (which includes CHW visits) shows strongest effects in lower-HDI countries (Cohen's d=0.66).

a3.label: Facility recall + defaulter tracing
a3.effect_measure: absolute percentage point increase in completion
a3.evidence_source: Indonesia cluster-RCT (HR 1.50 for DTPcv3); Cochrane review reminder-recall (OR 1.48); WHO defaulter tracing guidance
a3.evidence_quality: Moderate — fewer RCTs specific to defaulter tracing; evidence mainly from reminder-recall systems
a3.notes: CLAUDE.md default was +20-30%. CONFIRMED. Multi-component approaches (recall + outreach workers) have greater impact than single-component. Indonesia trial: 50% more likely to complete within 60 days.

a4.label: Conditional incentive
a4.effect_measure: absolute percentage point increase in completion
a4.evidence_source: New Incentives Nigeria RCT (+9-18pp); Banerjee et al. 2010 India (+22pp); Pakistan mCCT trial 2022; Meta-analysis 2025 (OR 1.34)
a4.evidence_quality: High — multiple RCTs including Nigeria-specific evidence (New Incentives)
a4.notes: CLAUDE.md default was +10-20%. Updated LOWER bound to 9% and UPPER to 22% based on New Incentives Nigeria RCT (9-18pp) and Banerjee India trial (22pp). Nigeria-specific evidence is strongest. Cost-effectiveness: $1500-6000 per death averted.

reward_function.notes: No changes recommended to CLAUDE.md defaults. Lambda=0.001 is consistent with published cost-effectiveness thresholds in LMIC vaccination programs.

changes_from_defaults.a4_range: Changed from 10-20% to 9-22% (New Incentives Nigeria RCT 9-18pp; Banerjee India 22pp)
